featured.title: WSJ Exposes Chinese Parts Inside America's Humanoid Robots — Congress Responds
featured.summary: The Wall Street Journal has revealed that key components inside many US-made humanoid robots originate from Chinese suppliers, raising national-security concerns as the technology approaches commercial deployment. Congress is reportedly preparing legislation modeled on the DJI drone ban, which could restrict supply chains for companies like Figure AI, Apptronik, and others. The disclosure puts the entire US humanoid robotics industry on notice: firms may need to rapidly re-shore or diversify component sourcing before regulatory pressure forces their hand.

articles.title: Torobo: Tokyo Robotics Unveils Bipedal Humanoid Rivaling Atlas
articles.summary: Tokyo Robotics has debuted Torobo, a bipedal humanoid that draws immediate comparisons to Boston Dynamics' Atlas in both form and movement quality. The company recently transitioned Torobo from a wheeled platform to a fully bipedal design, a significant engineering step that signals Japan's growing ambition in the humanoid robot race. The demo video circulating on r/robotics has attracted widespread attention from the global robotics community.

articles.title: UBTECH and Honda Trading Partner to Advance Humanoid Robots and Autonomous Vehicles
articles.summary: UBTECH Robotics has signed a strategic partnership with Honda Trading to accelerate the commercialization of humanoid robots and autonomous logistics vehicles. The deal combines UBTECH's Walker X platform with Honda Trading's extensive distribution and supply-chain networks across Asia and beyond. It marks another significant cross-industry alliance as automakers and robot makers seek to share manufacturing expertise and scale.

articles.title: Where Robotics Is Headed: AI, Humanoids and the Rise of Physical Intelligence
articles.summary: Assembly Magazine's sweeping industry analysis outlines the convergence of large language models and physical robotics into a new discipline called 'physical intelligence.' The report argues that 2026 marks the inflection point where robots transition from single-task machines to general-purpose workers capable of adapting to novel environments, drawing on insights from leading labs including Physical Intelligence (Pi) and Google DeepMind.

articles.title: Chinese Robotics Startup Seeking Chief Scientist at $18M Salary to Challenge Tesla Optimus
articles.summary: A Chinese humanoid robotics startup developing a direct rival to Tesla's Optimus is offering an $18 million annual salary to attract a world-class chief scientist, according to Business Insider. The extraordinary compensation package reflects intensifying competition for elite AI-robotics talent as Chinese firms race to close the gap with US counterparts. The unnamed company is reportedly backed by major state-aligned investors.

articles.title: Resilient Actuator Breakthrough Could Enable Soft Robots in Space
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a new class of actuator designed to withstand the extreme conditions of outer space — including vacuum, radiation, and temperature swings — without mechanical failure. The system uses a novel elastomer composite that maintains performance across a wider range than previous soft-robotic actuators, opening pathways for deployable robotic systems on future lunar and Mars missions. The work was published in a peer-reviewed materials science journal.

articles.title: What Amazon Saw in Fauna Robotics' Humanoid Strategy
articles.summary: The Robot Report has obtained insight into Amazon's strategic rationale for acquiring Fauna Robotics, maker of the Sprout humanoid. Amazon was drawn by Fauna's unique focus on dexterous, human-scale manipulation in unstructured environments — a capability gap that Amazon's existing warehouse robots cannot fill. The acquisition signals Amazon's intent to move beyond fixed-automation and deploy humanoids as flexible generalist workers across its fulfillment network.

articles.title: Autonomous Sequencing and Error Recovery in Robotic Construction
articles.summary: AZoBuild reports on new research demonstrating autonomous task sequencing and real-time error recovery for construction robots. The system can re-plan on the fly when components are misaligned or tools fail, a major step toward truly autonomous construction site operation. Field trials showed the robot completing multi-step masonry tasks with a 91% success rate without human intervention.

articles.title: Big-D Construction Expands Autonomous Robotics Deployment with FieldAI
articles.summary: US construction giant Big-D Construction has deepened its partnership with FieldAI, deploying autonomous robots across multiple active construction sites. The robots perform site surveys, progress monitoring, and safety inspections using AI-powered computer vision, reducing the need for manual walkthroughs. The rollout is one of the largest commercial robotics deployments in the US construction sector to date.

articles.title: Maximo's AI Robots Install Over 100MW of Solar Panels Using NVIDIA Tech
articles.summary: Maximo, an AI-powered construction robotics startup, has crossed the 100 megawatt milestone for solar panel installations performed by its autonomous robots. Highlighted during NVIDIA's National Robotics Week coverage, Maximo's robots use NVIDIA Isaac for perception and planning, enabling them to handle site variability and weather conditions. The achievement demonstrates that physical AI can now reliably scale renewable energy infrastructure.

articles.title: MIT Ultrasound Wristband Gives Robots Human-Level Dexterity
articles.summary: MIT researchers have developed an ultrasound-based wristband that captures fine muscle movements in the human forearm with millisecond precision, allowing those movements to be mapped directly onto robotic hands. In tests, a robot glove controlled by the wristband matched human dexterity scores on standardized manipulation tasks for the first time. The non-invasive wearable could become a key interface for teleoperation and robot training data collection.

articles.title: ForSight Robotics Performs World's First Fully Robotic Cataract Surgery
articles.summary: ForSight Robotics has announced the world's first fully autonomous, robot-performed cataract surgery using its JASPER platform. The procedure was completed under physician supervision with no manual intervention during the operation. The milestone is considered significant because cataract surgery requires sub-millimeter precision and is one of the highest-volume surgical procedures globally, representing a massive addressable market for medical robotics.

articles.title: Microsoft, NVIDIA and University of Washington Unveil OmniReset for Robotics AI
articles.summary: A joint team from Microsoft, NVIDIA, and the University of Washington has released OmniReset, an open-source framework that enables robotic systems to automatically recover from failures and reset to safe states during training and deployment. OmniReset addresses one of the key bottlenecks in sim-to-real transfer — the need for human resets when robots get stuck — and could dramatically accelerate reinforcement learning at scale.

articles.title: South Korea Eyes 2030 as Critical Window for Humanoid Robot Market Leadership
articles.summary: South Korean government officials and industry analysts have identified the 2026–2030 window as decisive for the country to establish a leadership position in the humanoid robot market. South Korea's strategy combines Samsung, Hyundai, and LG Group's manufacturing capabilities with aggressive R&D investment, aiming to challenge China and the US. DongA Science reports the government is preparing a multi-billion dollar robotics competitiveness fund.

articles.title: China Tests Humanoid Robots for Border Control Tasks with Vietnam
articles.summary: China has begun piloting humanoid robots in border patrol and control operations along its frontier with Vietnam, according to ECOticias. The robots are equipped with thermal cameras, facial recognition, and terrain-adaptive locomotion, and are being evaluated for checkpoint monitoring and perimeter surveillance. The deployment is among the first known uses of humanoid-form robots for national border security operations.

articles.title: US Largest Shipbuilder Teams with GrayMatter Robotics on Physical AI
articles.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries, the US Navy's largest shipbuilder, has partnered with GrayMatter Robotics to deploy physical AI systems for complex welding and assembly tasks on naval vessels. GrayMatter's robots use 3D vision and adaptive AI to handle the unstructured, variable conditions of shipbuilding — a historically difficult environment to automate. The deal underscores growing interest in robotics for defense-industrial manufacturing.

articles.title: BASF and Sevnce Robotics Partner for Smart Chemical Production Automation
articles.summary: Chemical giant BASF has signed a partnership with Sevnce Robotics to deploy autonomous inspection and sampling robots inside its chemical production facilities. The robots can handle corrosive and hazardous environments that are unsafe for human workers, performing routine checks and anomaly detection around the clock. The deal is part of BASF's broader digitalization strategy and highlights expanding robotics use in high-risk industrial sectors.

articles.title: Humanoid Robots Drive Tactile Sensor Development as Taiwan Firms Compete
articles.summary: Taiwan's semiconductor and electronics supply chain is pivoting toward tactile sensor development, driven by surging demand from humanoid robot manufacturers globally. Digitimes reports that firms including Merry Electronics and others are racing to develop high-density skin-like pressure sensors for robot hands. Taiwan's dominance in precision manufacturing gives it a structural advantage in supplying this emerging component category.

articles.title: Light-Powered Nanorobots Capture and Remove Bacteria from Water
articles.summary: Scientists have demonstrated nanorobots powered by visible light that can seek out and capture harmful bacteria in water samples with high efficiency. The robots use photocatalytic chemistry to both propel themselves and destroy pathogens on contact, requiring no external power source beyond ambient light. The breakthrough has potential applications in water purification, targeted drug delivery, and infection treatment.

articles.title: Serve Robotics Debuts Conversational AI Delivery Robot at NVIDIA GTC
articles.summary: Serve Robotics showcased a next-generation sidewalk delivery robot featuring conversational AI at NVIDIA GTC 2026. The robot can communicate with bystanders, respond to real-time questions about its delivery status, and handle complex pedestrian interactions using edge AI processing. Serve, which operates Uber Eats deliveries in Los Angeles, says the conversational capability improves safety and customer experience in dense urban environments.

articles.title: POSCO Group Invests 7 Billion Won in Robotics Automation Firm Brils
articles.summary: South Korean steel conglomerate POSCO Group has committed 7 billion Korean won (approximately $5 million USD) to Brils, a robotics automation startup specializing in heavy industrial applications. The investment will fund Brils' development of AI-driven robotic systems for steel mill operations, where extreme heat and precision requirements have long challenged automation efforts. It signals traditional heavy industry's accelerating adoption of next-generation robotics.
